idea.hook: Can we ever visit Andromeda, our galactic neighbor?
facts: Andromeda is 2.537 million light-years away from Earth.
If you traveled at light speed (299,792 km/s), it would take 2.537 million years to get there.
The fastest human-made object (New Horizons, ~59,000 km/h) would need about 46.4 billion years!
idea.payoff: Intergalactic travel remains a staggering challenge, requiring journeys longer than the universe has existed with current tech!
idea.title: Andromeda: Impossible Road Trip?! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: View of the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxy shown as two distinct spirals, with an arrow pointing between them.
Zoom out from Earth, showing a vast cosmic distance to the Andromeda galaxy. Text appears between the two galaxies.

scenes.text: At light speed (299,792 km/s): 2.537 MILLION years!
scenes.visual: A sleek, futuristic spaceship icon streaks from Earth towards Andromeda, leaving a bright light trail. A digital counter rapidly displays increasing years.
A small New Horizons probe icon slowly drifts across a vast cosmic background, with the Andromeda galaxy far in the distance. A huge numerical display for '46.4 Billion' flashes.

scenes.text: Longer than the universe has existed with current tech!
scenes.visual: A cosmic time warp effect, showing swirling galaxies and nebulae, with a clock-like visual ticking impossibly fast, emphasizing the scale of time.
